avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already exist in the early morning. Increase in danger in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Moist and wet avalanches can be released by people, especially on very steep west, north and east facing slopes below approximately 2500 m. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Individual natural avalanches are possible. Especially on steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.
Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften quickly. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.highlights: With the end of the rainfall, the natural activity of wet avalanches will gradually decrease.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of the rain more medium-sized wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular during the course of the night. Caution is to be exercised on very steep shady slopes. In steep gullies avalanches can reach areas without any snow cover. The runout zones are to be avoided.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Northwest, north and northeast facing slopes: The snowpack will be wet all the way through in all altitude zones. As a consequence of rain up to high altitudes the snowpack can not consolidate during the night.

West and east facing slopes: Only a little snow is now lying. The snowpack will be wet all the way through.

tendency.highlights: Gradual decrease in danger of wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wet avalanches can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant. Avalanches can in isolated cases release the saturated snowpack and reach medium size. As the day progresses the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase a little.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be wet all the way through over a wide area. The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning. In particular on steep sunny slopes as well as below approximately 1800 m only a little snow is now lying.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will increase a little during the day. Wet snow requires caution.

avalancheActivity.comment: Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular during the course of the night. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. In very isolated cases avalanches are medium-sized. In steep gullies they can reach areas without any snow cover. The runout zones are to be avoided.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Northwest, north and northeast facing slopes: The rain gave rise to extreme and thorough wetting of the snowpack. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be barely evident.

West and east facing slopes: Only a little snow is now lying. The snowpack will be wet all the way through.
